title: lachnanthes caroliana
intro: Lachnanthes caroliana, also known as yellow fringed pansy, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and fringed petals. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Lachnanthes caroliana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies for its purported medicinal properties.
